No end-to-end server test exists for the ?publisherId=<email> filter path (grep tests/server/ for publisherId+email returns 0 matches)

functional_blockers.title: board --publisher-email filter is broken end-to-end
functional_blockers.scope: src/lib/bounty/index.ts:226 (server list() SQL) + src/cli/commands/bounty-task/board.ts:113 (wire contract) + tests/cli/bounty-task-board-publisher-email.test.ts (incomplete coverage)
functional_blockers.description: CLI sends `?publisherId=<email>` on the wire (verified). Server's BountyService.list() SQL is `WHERE publisher_id = ?` comparing against tasks.publisher_id (UUID FK column). The server never translates the email to a UUID before the WHERE clause, so all matches return zero. The existing CLI test only mocks fetch and asserts URL contract — never asserts that the server returns matching tasks.
functional_blockers.evidence: Runtime test (since deleted): published 1 task as alice@example.com (UUID 8de9b6aa-...). GET /api/tasks?publisherId=alice@example.com → 0 tasks; GET /api/tasks?publisherId=8de9b6aa-... → 1 task.
functional_blockers.user_directive_clause: shared requireEmailFlag ... board --publisher-email filter and publisherId=<email>
functional_blockers.fix_recommendation: Server-side: in src/lib/bounty/index.ts list() and getBoard(), when filter.publisherId is supplied, if it contains '@' treat it as an email and resolve to agent.id via findAgentByEmail first; or rename the CLI wire to `?publisherEmail=<email>` and the server to filter on tasks.publisher_email column. Add server-side integration test that creates a task, then GETs the board with publisherId=<email> AND asserts len(result) == 1.
